title: MUSIC Know of any other music similar to the Refrain CD
display_title: MUSIC Know of any other music similar to the Refrain CD
wikitext:
[]: [MUSIC] Know of any other music similar to the Refrain CD?             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Mon Mar 02, 2009 5:52 am This album has grown on me severely in the six or so years I've had it. Favorite tracks are Kibou no Sora, Love Antique and Genwaku no umi kara. I want mooooore music like this, don't care what language. Bass-heavy, melodic, and lots of well-placed accompanying instruments that come and go. My least favorite track is probably Mugen houyou, though only due to the chorus.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Mar 02, 2009 1:56 pm There are actually two Refrain CDs: ~refrain~ The songs were inspired by Evangelion which is the one you're talking about, and Refrain of Evangelion (This is like a "Best Of" CD, it was released for Eva's 10 year anniversary). I've heard more of the later CD then the former myself, but I really like Yoko Takahashi's stuff (I've heard the original takes of her Eva songs as well as some of Hayashibara's versions) so I'll be sure to check this out. Also, call me crazy, but I kinda think some of S2 Works (S2=Shiro Sagisu) is similar to ~refrain~ . The only thing is that CD set has mostly background pieces from Eva and only a few of the songs.

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Mon Mar 02, 2009 4:54 pm S2 works is massive, though. the refrain (Eva-inspired) CD has a few neat mixes that I don't think are on any of the S2 CDs. Refrain's definitely a different shade of Eva's music, though.. actually, it isn't even really eva's music, per se, as Soul Refrain is the only track on there other than the title that finds itself into the series. it doesn't even sound a great deal like the more sublime songs on there.. I didn't know S2 was a play on his name, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Tue Mar 03, 2009 3:08 pm S2 works doesn't have any vocal pieces besides the songs from EoE. It's still fantastic, though.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Mar 03, 2009 4:30 pm Everything You've Ever Dreamed , that was scrapped from EoE doesn't count? :blink:

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Mar 03, 2009 5:58 pm Scrapped music for EoE is still EoE music. Yoko Takahashi's standalone stuff is probably similar to what's on "refrain". I can try to dig up some shitty MP3s I (probably still) have of this one album if somebody's interested. (I say shitty because they were poorly ripped and crackling sounds may bite your inner ear at any time.)

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Tue Mar 03, 2009 9:23 pm Ooooh, I'm interested.

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Tue Mar 03, 2009 10:13 pm Yoko didn't write the songs though, right? Shiro didn't, either.. anime vocal tracks tend to be collaborative to a point of annoyance. but yeah, I'd love some similar songs. Everything you ever dreamed is a nice track.

[]:             Quote Post by Soul Fire » Wed Mar 04, 2009 11:57 am I have nearly every single one of the evangelion music cd's and scores. I have the odd ones with the voice actors acting out some kind of role play on stage during a recess in the performance as well, though i havent been able to find translations for those particular tracks. Which cd is the Everything You've Ever Dreamed track on ?

[]:             Quote Post by majlund » Wed Mar 04, 2009 1:20 pm Soul Fire wrote: I have the odd ones with the voice actors acting out some kind of role play on stage during a recess in the performance as well, though i havent been able to find translations for those particular tracks.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Mar 04, 2009 1:23 pm majlund: Since he mentioned "recess in a performance", he's probably talking about the live concert album. "Everything You Ever Dreamed" was first released on "S2 Works", as well. Okay, here it is: Yoko Takahashi: Li La Three versions of NGE songs on there, by the way. (EDIT: I also feel justified in posting it because it's [more than likely] grotesquely out of print. The fact that there's only one crappy Dark Ages rip being swapped around gives it away. )

[]:             Quote Post by Oedipusfoot » Wed Mar 04, 2009 4:39 pm Reichu wrote: Okay, here it is: Yoko Takahashi: Li La

[]:             Quote Post by Soul Fire » Thu Mar 05, 2009 12:12 am Yes this is all very much appreciated. It has answered even the questions I forgot to ask about I hadnt heard the track that Reichu put up either. One of the few I havent got! Cheers

[]:             Quote Post by FallenTabris » Sun Mar 08, 2009 6:28 am Good stuff, thanks.
